title: Lifecycle, Storage Classes, Archive Restore, and Cost · Runtime Contract: Cost Model Changed Traffic
summary: A policy transitions active content immediately, expires current versions but not hidden history, and promises instant archive access without retrieval or minimum-duration cost. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cost model changed traffic increment in lifecycle and storage-cost decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Calculate storage, requests, transitions, retrieval, early deletion, transfer, CDN, invalidation, logs, and operational labor under changed traffic.
Resolve lifecycle expiration against versioning, Object Lock, legal hold, replication, business retention, and deletion evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Cost Model Changed Traffic · Theory Cost Model Changed Traffic
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7761 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to evaluate lifecycle transition, retention lock, restore, and minimum-duration cost; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
